title: Proxmox LVM-Thin Data and Metadata Utilization Guardrails
app: Proxmox TA or REST modular input
spl:
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:storage" type="lvmthin" earliest=-1h
| where data_pct>=80 OR meta_pct>=70
| stats latest(data_pct) as data_pct, latest(meta_pct) as meta_pct by storage, node
| sort -data_pct
description: LVM-thin provisioning on Proxmox allows oversubscription of guest disks. High data or metadata utilization on the thin pool risks immediate write failures for all guests on that pool even though individual VM graphs still look healthy.
value: Prevents thin-pool exhaustion incidents by monitoring pool-level metadata and data ratios, not only per-VM allocation.
implementation: Poll storage status per node and index `data` and `meta` utilization percentages returned by the API (field names vary slightly by version—normalize in the input). Alert at conservative thresholds (for example 80% data, 70% meta). Include runbook steps for `lvextend` and guest disk review.
visualization: Heatmap (node × storage), Table (pools breaching threshold), Single value (worst pool).
